business.headline: RBI MPC meeting starts today: Will rising inflation risks lead to a rate hike?The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) begins its three-day meeting today amid rising concerns over inflation, higher crude oil prices and global uncertainties. Will these factors be enough to trigger a rate hike?

Not correct: RBI rejects $12-billion gold sale report, says reserves unchangedIn a clarification issued on Tuesday, the RBI shared reports suggesting it had sold part of its gold holdings were "not correct" and highlighted that its physical stock of gold remains unchanged at 880.52 tonnes.

Want to use EPFO's UPI feature? Fix these 5 things firstImagine accessing your PF savings as easily as making a UPI payment. That's the experience EPFO is aiming to offer soon. However, the new feature will work smoothly only if your account details are accurate. Experts recommend checking five key areas now to avoid problems later.

Gold, silver prices are down again. Should investors buy, hold or wait?If you've been tracking gold and silver prices recently, you've probably noticed that the rally has lost some steam. Both metals have slipped despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, leaving investors with an important decision to make: buy more, stay invested, or wait for the dust to settle.

TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech crash up to 8%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell sharply on Wednesday as investors booked profits after a two-day rally. Weak ADR cues, cooling global software momentum and uncertainty over AI gains weighed on sentiment.

US proposes up to 12.5% tariff on India, China over forced labour concernsThe proposed action, announced by the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR), comes under Section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974, the same law that was used to impose tariffs on Chinese imports during President Donald Trump's first term.

Sensex crashes 700 points. What's behind today's market meltdown?Markets opened sharply lower as renewed Iran-US tensions, higher crude prices and heavy IT profit-booking hit trade on Wednesday. The weaker rupee, persistent FPI outflow concerns and the RBI policy outlook kept volatility elevated.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade over crude oil price rise; TCS falls 6%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 763.45 points, or 1.02%, to 73,886.39 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 213.75 points, or 0.91%, to 23,269.80.

IndusInd Bank faces fresh whistleblower allegations; complaint sent to PMO, RBIA whistleblower complaint has sought a probe into alleged insider trading and governance lapses at IndusInd Bank. The allegations add fresh scrutiny as the lender remains under watch over its Rs 2,000-crore derivatives accounting discrepancy.

Not just the AI king. Nvidia may be the world's safest companyNvidia's credit default swap has traded close to US sovereign levels as its market value and earnings surge. The reading reflects unusually strong confidence in the AI chipmaker's ability to withstand shocks and meet obligations.

US wants to end Russian oil sanctions waiver: How it will hit IndiaThe US has repeatedly criticised purchases of Russian oil since the Ukraine conflict began, claiming that revenues from crude exports help fund Moscow's military campaign.

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Oyo parent Prism gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650 crore IPO, targets up to $8 billion valuationFollowing Sebi's approval, the company is now expected to file an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-1), which will be made available for public review and comments.

Waiting for Form 16? Here's when you'll receive it and why it matters for your ITRThe countdown to ITR filing has begun, and Form 16 remains one of the most important documents for salaried taxpayers. Knowing when your employer must issue it and what information it contains can help you prepare for a smoother tax filing experience.

Sensex ends 382 points higher, Nifty nears 23,500 led by IT sector rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 382.50 points, or 0.52%, to close at 74,649.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 100.95 points, or 0.43%, to settle at 23,483.55.

From 5th to 7th: Why India is losing the stock market race against Asian rivalsIndia has slipped from fifth to seventh in global stock market rankings as South Korea and Taiwan moved ahead. The shift shows investors are favouring AI-linked semiconductor hubs over markets with limited listed chipmakers.

8th Pay Commission: Final date to submit salary and pension demands extendedAnother extension, more questions. The 8th Pay Commission has pushed back its submission deadline again, keeping employees guessing about pay hikes and arrears.

Will your home loan EMI rise again? What RBI's June MPC meet could mean for borrowersIf you have a home loan, the RBI's June policy meeting could be worth watching closely. Borrowers are eager to know whether their EMIs will remain stable or face another change in the months ahead.

Why is Nvidia hiring when the rest of Silicon Valley is firingNvidia is expanding H-1B hiring even as major technology rivals cut jobs and slow foreign recruitment. The move stands out for Indian professionals facing layoffs, visa pressure and a tighter US market.

Why your society maintenance bill could soon be Rs 3,000 higher every monthFor many apartment residents, the next surprise could arrive not in their power bill or fuel bill, but in their society maintenance statement. Rising wage costs and higher operating expenses are forcing several housing societies to consider significant fee hikes.

entertainment.headline: Dinesh Vijan jokes Shahid Kapoor's love life was a hexagon. Actor responds At the Cocktail 2 trailer launch, Dinesh Vijan joked that Shahid Kapoor's love life was a hexagon, not a triangle. This invited another candid remark from the actor, who referenced that it was rather a circle.

Don 3 row: Producers body cites Akshaye Khanna's Drishyam 3 exit, discusses conduct Amid Ranveer Singh's Don 3 row, the Producers Guild of India has cited complaints about Akshaye Khanna's departure from Drishyam 3. The actor's exit came amid Dhurandhar success.

The Voice of Hind Rajab receives censor certificate after months in limbo The Voice of Hind Rajab, a film depicting the tragic killing of a Palestinian child during the Israel-Gaza conflict, has finally received its censor certificate in India after months of delay.

Was Ranveer Singh right to serve legal notice on FWICE? What industry, lawyers say The Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) has withdrawn its non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh after the actor issued a legal notice against the film body. As Don 3 impasse heats up, lawyers and a veteran industry voice weigh in.

Ranveer Singh scores first win in Don 3 row as film body withdraws 'ban' The Federation of Western Cine Employees lifted the non-cooperation directive issued against actor Ranveer Singh following his abrupt exit from Don 3. The film body further said they would fight Singh's legal notice in court.

Pankaj Bhadouria shares recovery update, urges women to get breast cancer screening Pankaj Bhadouria shared a video update after her breast cancer surgery and thanked supporters for their prayers. She said early detection helped her recovery and urged women to get regular screenings and check-ups.

Jennifer Lopez approves Mika Abdalla's Off Campus dance tribute: Obsessed Jennifer Lopez said she loved Mika Abdalla's Off Campus dance to On The Floor and called it very clever. The viral scene has pushed the song back onto the charts as the series prepares to centre Allie and Dean in season two.

What if Dhurandhar had flopped? Inside Ritesh Sidhwani's showdown with Ranveer A new report about the Don 3 dispute suggests that Ranveer Singh exited the film after Dhurandhar's success and admitted that he wouldn't have left the film otherwise. The disclosures have intensified the dispute as he challenges FWICE's non-cooperation directive in court.

Actor Idris Elba is now Sir Idris Elba after King Charles grants knighthood King Charles III knighted Idris Elba at Windsor Castle for his services to young people. The honour recognises both his contribution to entertainment and his longstanding work empowering young people.

From Bridgerton to You: Netflix launches 'watch your favourite books' hub With 'Watch Your Favourite Books', Netflix has brought its book-inspired films and series together in one place. The section is grouped by reader interests, including romance, history and comics.

Detained for 47 days, deported to India: Comic shares Dubai ordeal over comedy Reel Comedian Yash Bhardwaj said Dubai authorities detained him for 47 days over a comedy reel. He said the episode ended with his deportation to India, which deeply affected his family.

Karuppu director RJ Balaji meets CM Vijay, credits him for 'starting it all' Director RJ Balaji shared photographs from his meeting with Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and thanked him for first hearing Karuppu. The post brought back how Vijay's feedback and eventual exit helped the film finally reach Suriya.

Producer Nikhil Dwivedi backs court route amid Ranveer-Farhan's Don 3 dispute Nikhil Dwivedi has weighed in on the Don 3 dispute involving Ranveer Singh, Excel Entertainment and FWICE. He said industry bodies can mediate, but contractual rows must be settled in court.

Made in India A Titan Story review: A tribute to vision, people and nation's prideRelease Date:Mon Jun 01, 2026 In an era of flashy gadgets and instant gratification, Naseeruddin Shah and Jim Sarbh's Made in India: A Titan Story takes you back to a time when a simple wristwatch carried dreams of pride, precision, and possibility. Does the show do justice to the legacy of the most successful consumer brand? Here is our review.

Ranveer Singh chose backend deal over big fee for Dhurandhar, says producer Producer Jyoti Deshpande said actor Ranveer Singh took a lower fixed fee for Dhurandhar and earned more through a backend deal. She said the model shared risk and reward, even as the film grew from one part to two parts.

Ram Charan's Peddi gets special show, Telangana government approves ticket hike The Telangana government has allowed higher ticket rates and a June 3 premiere show for Ram Charan's Peddi. The order puts the film on course for release with revised pricing in both Telugu states and strong advance sales.

26-year-old man dies, 3 injured during blast sequence on SJ Suryah's Killer set A gas cylinder allegedly exploded during a bomb-blast sequence on the set of SJ Suryah's Killer in Chennai. The blast killed a 26-year-old crew member, injured three others and prompted a safety enquiry.

Not Suitable for Work review: Mindy Kaling's Gen Z comedy feels datedRelease Date:Tue Jun 02, 2026 Mindy Kaling's Not Suitable for Work follows five young professionals through work, friendship and dating chaos in Manhattan. The review finds it polished and mildly entertaining, but too dated to fully capture Gen Z adulthood.

Peddi box office prediction: Rs 100 crore opening and Ram Charan's game changing bet Trade analysts expect Peddi to open strongly, driven by the Telugu market and overseas sales. The film's hold beyond day one could determine Ram Charan's standing as a solo box-office draw.

general news.headline: 21 killed in Delhi hotel fire, 2 women seen jumping off building UpdatesSources said the hotel had been granted a licence by the Delhi government under the Bed and Breakfast scheme to operate six rooms, but was allegedly running 25 rooms.

TMC rift: 60 rebel MLAs back expelled leader as LoP, but want Mamata as head  Live UpdatesWith the backing of 60 legislators, Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha would comfortably cross the two-thirds threshold required under the anti-defection law. This could potentially allow their faction to stake a claim to the TMC's name and symbol if a formal split occurs and Mamata Banerjee rejects their proposal.

Delhi fire: Malviya Nagar B&B was licensed for 6 rooms. It built 25India Today TV has learnt that the Flourish Stay hotel was licensed to operate only six rooms under the Bed and Breakfast scheme but was allegedly running as many as 25 rooms.

Ranveer Singh scores first win in Don 3 row as film body withdraws 'ban'The Federation of Western Cine Employees lifted the non-cooperation directive issued against actor Ranveer Singh following his abrupt exit from Don 3. The film body further said they would fight Singh's legal notice in court.

CBSE website lesson: Indian digital infra is not ready for AI tools like MythosThe fiasco with CBSE website and its On Screen Marking (OSM) system has jeopardised the future of thousands of Class 12 students. Yet, it can also teach a lesson to the country if our government is willing to learn. It is a lesson that may come handy in the AI era when tools like Mythos arrive.

Live: DK Shivakumar takes mother's blessings ahead of swearing-in as Karnataka CMKarnataka CM Swearing-In Live Updates: DK Shivakumar, former Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister and a long-time troubleshooter for the Congress in southern India, is set to take oath as Chief Minister today. The ceremony is scheduled for 4 pm at Lok Bhavan. Sources indicate the party may opt for a two- to three-deputy chief minister formula. Around 10 to 13 others are also expected to be sworn in as part of the new cabinet. All eyes will be on the event as a new chapter unfolds in Karnataka. Follow this space for updates. Karnataka CM Swearing-In Live Updates: DK Shivakumar, former Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister and a long-time troubleshooter for the Congress in southern India, is set to take oath as Chief Minister today. The ceremony is scheduled for 4 pm at Lok Bhavan. Sources indicate the party may opt for a two- to three-deputy chief minister formula. Around 10 to 13 others are also expected to be sworn in as part of the new cabinet. All eyes will be on the event as a new chapter unfolds in Karnataka. Follow this space for updates.

Video: Two women jump from burning Delhi hotel, one was carrying a childA video shows two women jumping from the burning hotel onto mattresses laid out by locals, who rushed to help. One woman briefly got to her feet before collapsing again. According to an eyewitness, one of the women was carrying a child when she landed.

Who's Ritabrata Banerjee, once Left's blue-eyed boy, now TMC's Eknath Shinde?Once celebrated as the Left's blue-eyed boy, Ritabrata Banerjee was in 2018 welcomed into the Trinamool Congress fold. Today, he stands at the centre of a rebellion that could see TMC founder Mamata Banerjee lose control of her party. All about Ritabrata, who could end up being Bengal's Eknath Shinde.

Was Ranveer Singh right to serve legal notice on FWICE? What industry, lawyers sayThe Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) has withdrawn its non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh after the actor issued a legal notice against the film body. As Don 3 impasse heats up, lawyers and a veteran industry voice weigh in.

Live: 18 Trinamool MLAs attend Bengal CM Suvendu's meetBengal TMC Rift Live Updates: The buzz around the internal rift within West Bengal's principal Opposition party, the Trinamool Congress (TMC), grew significantly louder on Wednesday as MLA Ritabrata Banerjee, who was expelled by the Mamata Banerjee-led camp on charges of anti-party activities, reached the state Assembly and claimed that he has the support of 60 of the party's 80 MLAs. However, the rebel faction maintained Mamata was still their party leader. Follow IndiaToday.in for all the latest updates on the TMC internal rift.

Shivakumar as Shiva on Karnataka newspaper frontpage, people call it blasphemyKannada newspaper Vishwavani triggered a row and is drawing criticism after depicting DK Shivakumar as Lord Shiva on its front page ahead of his oath-taking ceremony. The illustration has sparked outrage, with critics calling it an act of blasphemy and that of political flattery.

Rs 1 crore cash, bedrooms, gun, condoms in Kolkata college: Tip of the iceberg?Kolkata's Surendranath College, a prestigious institution deeply intertwined with the freedom movement, has found itself engulfed in a massive controversy after cash, liquor bottles and a revolver were recovered. It has brought into focus the issue of alleged misuse of power in Bengal educational institutions.

What if Dhurandhar had flopped? Inside Ritesh Sidhwani's showdown with RanveerA new report about the Don 3 dispute suggests that Ranveer Singh exited the film after Dhurandhar's success and admitted that he wouldn't have left the film otherwise. The disclosures have intensified the dispute as he challenges FWICE's non-cooperation directive in court.

8,056 deaths in five days: UP has the highest fatality rate on hot daysThat number is more than double the projected toll in Bihar, the second most affected state, which could see around 3,615 deaths.

4 months on, Khamenei's body to be carried in funeral procession before burialNearly four months after his assassination, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's body will be taken out for a funeral procession. Khamenei will be laid to rest in Iran's holy city of Mashhad, beside Imam Reza, in keeping with the late Ayatollah's final wish. Over 20 million mourners are likely to attend his funeral.

Indians with H1-B visa note: Google, Amazon hiring numbers reduced by up to 2900H-1B hiring by some of the biggest US tech companies is slowing as immigration policies tighten under the Trump administration. While Google and Amazon have cut approved foreign hires, Nvidia is continuing to expand its recruitment efforts amid the AI boom.

Neapolitan pizza on carts? Inside Noida's midnight gourmet street food sceneWhen it comes to gourmet wood-fired pizza in Delhi-NCR, names like Leo's, Da Susy, and Evoo are often the first that come to mind. But here's a surprise: street-side carts in Noida are now serving their own gourmet takes on pizza, bringing artisanal flavours and wood-fired-style creations to the city's bustling food scene.

French Open Live Updates: Kalinskaya eyes semis berth, Sabalenka in action laterFrench Open 2026 Day 11 Live Updates: Anna Kalinskaya looks to continue her impressive French Open run as the Russian takes on surprise quarter-finalist Maja Chwalinska for a place in the Roland Garros semi-finals.

What is a flex-fuel vehicle, and can it help you save money?As India accelerates its transition towards cleaner and more energy-secure mobility, flex-fuel vehicles are emerging as an alternative. Capable of running on petrol, ethanol, or a combination of both, these vehicles could help reduce oil imports while offering a potentially cheaper and greener transportation solution. Is there any cost benefit for the consumers? Let's find out.

TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech crash up to 8%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell sharply on Wednesday as investors booked profits after a two-day rally. Weak ADR cues, cooling global software momentum and uncertainty over AI gains weighed on sentiment.
As petrol gets pricier, India's electric future comes into focusElectric passenger vehicle sales jumped nearly 50% year-on-year in May, outpacing petrol and diesel vehicles.

UPSC has built a leakproof island. Here's how it prevents paper leaksThe Supreme Court, while slamming the NTA over the NEET paper leak, praised the UPSC for its ability to conduct large-scale examinations without scandals. What exactly does the UPSC do differently? What do the experts say? Does that mean the UPSC model is foolproof?

Not correct: RBI rejects $12-billion gold sale report, says reserves unchangedIn a clarification issued on Tuesday, the RBI shared reports suggesting it had sold part of its gold holdings were "not correct" and highlighted that its physical stock of gold remains unchanged at 880.52 tonnes.

DK Shivakumar to be sworn in as Karnataka CM, 13 MLAs likely to take oathA long political journey reaches its defining moment as DK Shivakumar prepares to take oath as Karnataka Chief Minister, opening a new chapter in the state's politics after Siddaramaiah's exit.

sports.headline: Chasing immortality: The legendary veterans eyeing a final FIFA World Cup FIFA World Cup 2026: The countdown is on for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where a golden generation of veterans faces its final stand. From Luka Modrić to Mohamed Salah, these ageing masters must defy Father Time and brutal summer heat for one last shot at glory.

Satwik's injury halts Sat-Chi at Indonesia Open days after ending title drought Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty retired midway through their Indonesia Open opener with Satwik clutching his right shoulder. The setback came two days after their Singapore Open title, shifting focus to recovery before major events.

French Open Live Updates: Kalinskaya eyes semis berth, Sabalenka in action later French Open 2026 Day 11 Live Updates: Anna Kalinskaya looks to continue her impressive French Open run as the Russian takes on surprise quarter-finalist Maja Chwalinska for a place in the Roland Garros semi-finals.

F1 2026: Charles Leclerc extends stay with Ferrari, signs multi-year extension Ferrari has tied Charles Leclerc down to a new multi-year deal ahead of his home race in Monaco. Leclerc has been the best driver for Ferrari this season and is challenging Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli and George Russell.

Did Vaibhav Sooryavanshi insult Harsha Bhogle? Commentator calls out viral clip Harsha Bhogle has dismissed a viral video claiming Vaibhav Sooryavanshi insulted Virat Kohli. The clip depicts Sooryavanshi as brash and arrogant but Bhogle called out the creators for their attempt to tarnish the young prodigy's reputation.

Hype the entire Women's T20 World Cup, not just IND vs PAK: Smriti Mandhana India vice-captain Smriti Mandhana urged fans and media to hype the entire Women's T20 World Cup rather than focusing solely on the high-profile opening group match against rivals Pakistan.

Haryana's wrestling monks: How one state claimed all 18 Asian Games spots Haryana's wrestlers have claimed every available place in India's Asian Games squad after dominating the trials. The sweep from the athletes shows how the state, deep-rooted in their traditional methods, can dominate the rest.

India to play biggest-ever bilateral tour of New Zealand in landmark year India will tour New Zealand for a 12-match all-format series from October 22 to December 1. The tour will also mark 100 years of sporting ties between both nations.

Shreyas Iyer's misery continues as Shivam Dube steals the show in Mumbai T20 League Shreyas Iyer's batting struggles continued in the Mumbai T20 League opener, where he was dismissed for just five runs by Shivam Dube, triggering a major batting collapse for his team.

French Open Day 11 Schedule: Sabalenka in action, Indian talent on double duty World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka and an all-Italian night session headline the French Open quarterfinals, while Indian stars Sriram Balaji and Arnav Paparkar chase historic wins in Paris.

Meet Rasikh Salam: The boy Irfan Pathan spotted. The man RCB trusted IPL 2026: Irfan Pathan saw something in a teenager walking away from a trial in Srinagar. Eight years, four franchises and a two-year ban later, Rasikh Salam took 19 wickets in a title-winning campaign for Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Here is his story.

Pat Cummins hints at skipping IPL 2027: Tests, World Cup are my prioritiesPat Cummins has indicated he could skip IPL 2027 as Australia head into a punishing run of Tests and a World Cup. Cummins played just 8 games for SRH during the IPL 2026 season.

Fonseca far from reaching limits as dream run ends in French Open quarterfinalsJakub Mensik beat Joao Fonseca in straight sets to reach his first Grand Slam semi-final at Roland Garros. Fonseca said the defeat still strengthened his belief in his game, body and mentality.

Praggnanandhaa beats Carlsen twice, matching Vishy Anand's 19-year-old featHistory repeats itself as Indian GM Praggnanandhaa defeats World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen twice in a single classical tournament, joining Viswanathan Anand in one of chess's most exclusive clubs.

Zverev overcomes teen scare to enter French Open semi-finalsFrench Open: Second seed Alexander Zverev stormed into the semi-finals of Roland Garros after beating talented 19-year-old Rafael Jodar. The Spaniard gave Zverev a run for his money in the opening set but faded out later.

Suryakumar Yadav's poor run continues, fails in Mumbai T20 League after IPLIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav's struggles continued outside the Indian Premier League after the batter failed to assert his dominance in the city-based Mumbai T20 league. Chasing 210 runs vs Ajinkya Rahane's North Mumbai Panthers, Suryakumar got out for just 19 runs.

Sachin Tendulkar analyses IPL 2026 in detail, advocates new powerplay ruleLegendary Sachin Tendulkar has analysed the Indian Premier League's 2026 season in detail. Taking to Reddit, Sachin has opened up on the experience of watching Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the distinct identities the teams have taken, and has even advocated a new powerplay rule.

Kostyuk dedicates maiden Grand Slam semi-final to difficult nights in UkraineUkraine's Marta Kostyuk gave an emotional speech after reaching her first Grand Slam semi-final. Kostyuk beat her compatriot Elina Svitolina in a 3-set battle to reach the semis of the French Open.

Lakshya Sen knocked out in first round, Kidambi Srikanth's horror run continuesPV Sindhu opened with a straight-games win at the Indonesia Open as India faltered elsewhere. Her return to the top 10 stood out on a day of early exits across singles and doubles.

Gautam Gambhir's coaching unit gets new hand for young spinnersThe BCCI has appointed Sairaj Bahutule as India's spin-bowling coach under Gautam Gambhir. He will mentor a young group of spinners as India try to rebuild their home Test edge.

Snoop Dogg's gospel: Why 19-year-old Andreeva always thanks herself at SlamsMirra Andreeva swept past Sorana Cirstea to reach another French Open semi-final and then explained her habit of thanking herself after big wins. The victory added a teenage Roland Garros record to her run.

PV Sindhu returns to top 10 in badminton rankings after nearly 3 yearsPV Sindhu returned to the world top 10 and beat Busanan Ongbamrungphan in straight games at the Indonesia Open. The result reinforced her recent revival before a fresh test against world No. 1 An Se-young.

India favourites for T20 World Cup: Ex-Indian captain makes bold claimWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Anjum Chopra has backed India to begin the T20 World Cup in England as one of the favourites. Her view rests on the squad's growing familiarity with conditions and the need for a more flexible batting order.

Messi vs Ronaldo? Football's biggest debate gets one last World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: As Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo prepare for what could be their final World Cup, football gets one last chance to celebrate the rivalry that defined an era.

Quarterfinals Highlights: Zverev moves into semis with clinical show vs JodarFrench Open 2026 Highlights: 7th seed Elena Svitolina has crashed out of the French Open after losing to 23-year-old Marta Kostyuk in the quarter-final of the tournament. Meanwhile, Alexander Zverev beats Rafael Jodar in straight sets to move into his 5th Roland Garros semi-final.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland face tricky continental groupFIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland head a volatile Group B featuring Bosnia and Herzegovina, Canada and Qatar. The section will turn on veteran leaders, injury concerns and very little margin for error.

Man City's Rodri to decide future after World Cup as Real Madrid interest growsFIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri has parked questions over his Manchester City future until after Spain's World Cup campaign. His message to Spain is clear: work humbly, chase every ball, and ignore the noise.

After selection uproar, Auqib Nabi joins India's Test squad but with a twistIND vs AFG One-off Test: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Test squad sparked widespread debate, but the BCCI has now handed the Jammu and Kashmir pacer an unexpected opportunity, offering a twist to the selection story.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find himIPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
